texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: FinRL-DeepSeek: LLM-Infused Risk-Sensitive Reinforcement Learning for Trading Agents
texts.text: FinRL-DeepSeek: LLM-Infused Risk-Sensitive Reinforcement Learning for Trading Agents

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Mostapha Benhenda
texts.text: Mostapha Benhenda

texts.label: text
texts.orig: mostaphabenhenda@gmail.com
texts.text: mostaphabenhenda@gmail.com

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Abstract
texts.text: Abstract

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This paper presents a novel risk-sensitive trading agent combining reinforcement learning and large language models (LLMs). We extend the Conditional Value-at-Risk Proximal Policy Optimization (CPPO) algorithm, by adding risk assessment and trading recommendation signals generated by a LLM from financial news. Our approach is backtested on the Nasdaq-100 index benchmark, using financial news data from the FNSPID dataset and the DeepSeek V3, Qwen 2.5 and Llama 3.3 language models. The code, data, and trading agents are available at: https://github.com/benstaf/ FinRL_DeepSeek
texts.text: This paper presents a novel risk-sensitive trading agent combining reinforcement learning and large language models (LLMs). We extend the Conditional Value-at-Risk Proximal Policy Optimization (CPPO) algorithm, by adding risk assessment and trading recommendation signals generated by a LLM from financial news. Our approach is backtested on the Nasdaq-100 index benchmark, using financial news data from the FNSPID dataset and the DeepSeek V3, Qwen 2.5 and Llama 3.3 language models. The code, data, and trading agents are available at: https://github.com/benstaf/ FinRL_DeepSeek

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Introduction
texts.text: 1. Introduction

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Automated trading agents increasingly leverage reinforcement learning (RL), but often overlook two aspects:
texts.text: Automated trading agents increasingly leverage reinforcement learning (RL), but often overlook two aspects:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Integration of alternative data sources, such as financial news.
texts.text: Integration of alternative data sources, such as financial news.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. Risk management.
texts.text: Risk management.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This paper introduces a novel hybrid RL-LLM trading agent, incorporating financial news insights at both the action and risk levels. The main contribution of the paper is the introduction of an LLM-based risk assessment score derived from news, in addition to LLM-based trading recommendations. This demonstrates the potential of LLMs for feature extraction from news beyond standard sentiment analysis, achieved through carefully designed prompts.
texts.text: This paper introduces a novel hybrid RL-LLM trading agent, incorporating financial news insights at both the action and risk levels. The main contribution of the paper is the introduction of an LLM-based risk assessment score derived from news, in addition to LLM-based trading recommendations. This demonstrates the potential of LLMs for feature extraction from news beyond standard sentiment analysis, achieved through carefully designed prompts.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Related Work
texts.text: 2. Related Work

texts.label: text
texts.orig: There is a growing body of work on LLM-augmented RL agents, such as (Unnikrishnan, 2024; Anonymous, 2025). Compared with them, the way LLMs influence the RL agent is simpler here.
texts.text: There is a growing body of work on LLM-augmented RL agents, such as (Unnikrishnan, 2024; Anonymous, 2025). Compared with them, the way LLMs influence the RL agent is simpler here.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In FinGPT (Yang et al., 2023), RL is applied to the training of LLM on stock prices. This approach is more complex than ours, which has the benefit of leveraging established LLM APIs.
texts.text: In FinGPT (Yang et al., 2023), RL is applied to the training of LLM on stock prices. This approach is more complex than ours, which has the benefit of leveraging established LLM APIs.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Other hybrid RL-LLM approaches include FinCon (Yu et al., 2024), which makes a complex distillation of information through a synthesized multi-agent collaboration mechanism. In this paper, we only use simple prompts.
texts.text: Other hybrid RL-LLM approaches include FinCon (Yu et al., 2024), which makes a complex distillation of information through a synthesized multi-agent collaboration mechanism. In this paper, we only use simple prompts.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: These RL-LLM approaches also differ from 'pure LLM' agents like (Lopez-Lira and Tang, 2024) and (Ding et al., 2024) that only use LLM recommendations.
texts.text: These RL-LLM approaches also differ from 'pure LLM' agents like (Lopez-Lira and Tang, 2024) and (Ding et al., 2024) that only use LLM recommendations.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Data and LLM Prompts
texts.text: 3. Data and LLM Prompts

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We use the FNSPID dataset (Dong et al., 2024), which contains 15.7 million time-aligned financial news records spanning 1999-2023. To reduce LLM API costs, we randomly select a representative news article per stock per day, reducing the dataset to 2 million records. Using LLMs (DeepSeek V3 (DeepSeek-AI et al., 2024), Qwen 2.5 72B (Qwen et al., 2025) Llama 3.3 70B (Grattafiori et al., 2024)), we extract stock recommendations and risk assessment using the following prompts :
texts.text: We use the FNSPID dataset (Dong et al., 2024), which contains 15.7 million time-aligned financial news records spanning 1999-2023. To reduce LLM API costs, we randomly select a representative news article per stock per day, reducing the dataset to 2 million records. Using LLMs (DeepSeek V3 (DeepSeek-AI et al., 2024), Qwen 2.5 72B (Qwen et al., 2025) Llama 3.3 70B (Grattafiori et al., 2024)), we extract stock recommendations and risk assessment using the following prompts :

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Stock Recommendation Prompt (from (Dong et al., 2024)):
texts.text: Stock Recommendation Prompt (from (Dong et al., 2024)):

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 'You are a financial expert with stock recommendation experience. Based on a specific stock, score for range from 1 to 5, where 1 is negative, 2 is somewhat negative, 3 is neutral, 4 is somewhat positive, 5 is positive'
texts.text: 'You are a financial expert with stock recommendation experience. Based on a specific stock, score for range from 1 to 5, where 1 is negative, 2 is somewhat negative, 3 is neutral, 4 is somewhat positive, 5 is positive'

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Risk Assessment Prompt (new):
texts.text: Risk Assessment Prompt (new):

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 'You are a financial expert specializing in risk assessment for stock recommendations. Based on a specific stock, provide a risk score from 1 to 5, where: 1 indicates very low risk, 2 indicates low risk, 3 indicates moderate risk (default if the news lacks any clear indication of risk), 4 indicates high risk, and 5 indicates very high risk.'
texts.text: 'You are a financial expert specializing in risk assessment for stock recommendations. Based on a specific stock, provide a risk score from 1 to 5, where: 1 indicates very low risk, 2 indicates low risk, 3 indicates moderate risk (default if the news lacks any clear indication of risk), 4 indicates high risk, and 5 indicates very high risk.'

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4. Trading Algorithms
texts.text: 4. Trading Algorithms

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4.1. Reinforcement Learning agents on price-only data
texts.text: 4.1. Reinforcement Learning agents on price-only data

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4.1.1. PROXIMAL POLICY OPTIMIZATION (PPO)
texts.text: 4.1.1. PROXIMAL POLICY OPTIMIZATION (PPO)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: OpenAI (Schulman et al., 2017) introduced Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) reinforcement learning algorithm, and (Lele et al., 2020; Liu et al., 2022) applied it to stock trading
texts.text: OpenAI (Schulman et al., 2017) introduced Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) reinforcement learning algorithm, and (Lele et al., 2020; Liu et al., 2022) applied it to stock trading

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The PPO objective ensures stable policy updates by clipping the probability ratio:
texts.text: The PPO objective ensures stable policy updates by clipping the probability ratio:

texts.label: formula
texts.orig: L PPO ( θ ) = E [min ( r t ( θ ) · A t , clip ( r t ( θ ) , 1 -ϵ, 1 + ϵ ) · A t )]

texts.label: text
texts.orig: where:
texts.text: where:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · r t ( θ ) = π θ ( a t | s t ) π θ old ( a t | s t ) is the probability ratio between the new policy π θ and the old policy π θ old .
texts.text: r t ( θ ) = π θ ( a t | s t ) π θ old ( a t | s t ) is the probability ratio between the new policy π θ and the old policy π θ old .

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · A t is the advantage estimate at time t .
texts.text: A t is the advantage estimate at time t .

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · ϵ is the clipping parameter that restricts large updates to the policy.
texts.text: ϵ is the clipping parameter that restricts large updates to the policy.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4.1.2. CONDITIONAL VALUE AT RISK-PROXIMAL POLICY OPTIMIZATION (CVAR-PPO)
texts.text: 4.1.2. CONDITIONAL VALUE AT RISK-PROXIMAL POLICY OPTIMIZATION (CVAR-PPO)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Conditional Value at Risk-Proximal Policy Optimization (CVaR-PPO) (Ying et al., 2022) extends PPO with a risk constraint to penalize high-loss trajectories:
texts.text: Conditional Value at Risk-Proximal Policy Optimization (CVaR-PPO) (Ying et al., 2022) extends PPO with a risk constraint to penalize high-loss trajectories:

texts.label: formula
texts.orig: L CVaR-PPO ( θ, η, λ ) = L PPO ( θ )+ λ ( 1 1 -α E [( η -D ( π θ )) + ] -η + β ) (1)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: where:
texts.text: where:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · L PPO ( θ ) is the PPO objective.
texts.text: L PPO ( θ ) is the PPO objective.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · D ( π θ ) is the trajectory return.
texts.text: D ( π θ ) is the trajectory return.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · η is the CVaR threshold.
texts.text: η is the CVaR threshold.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · ( η -D ( π θ )) + = max(0 , η -D ( π θ )) represents the CVaR loss beyond the threshold.
texts.text: ( η -D ( π θ )) + = max(0 , η -D ( π θ )) represents the CVaR loss beyond the threshold.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · λ is the Lagrange multiplier enforcing the constraint.
texts.text: λ is the Lagrange multiplier enforcing the constraint.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · α is the CVaR confidence level (e.g., 0.05 for the worst 5%).
texts.text: α is the CVaR confidence level (e.g., 0.05 for the worst 5%).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · β is an auxiliary penalty parameter.
texts.text: β is an auxiliary penalty parameter.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To our knowledge, we are the first to apply CVaR-PPO to stock trading.
texts.text: To our knowledge, we are the first to apply CVaR-PPO to stock trading.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4.2. LLM-infused PPO
texts.text: 4.2. LLM-infused PPO

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In LLM-infused PPO, we compute stock-specific recommendation scores ( S f ) from the FNSPID dataset using an LLM. These scores influence trading actions. The influenced action is:
texts.text: In LLM-infused PPO, we compute stock-specific recommendation scores ( S f ) from the FNSPID dataset using an LLM. These scores influence trading actions. The influenced action is:

texts.label: formula
texts.orig: a mod t = S f · a t

texts.label: text
texts.orig: where:
texts.text: where:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · S f > 1 : Amplifies actions under positive recommendation.
texts.text: S f > 1 : Amplifies actions under positive recommendation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · S f < 1 : Dampens actions under negative recommendation.
texts.text: S f < 1 : Dampens actions under negative recommendation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · S f = 1 : Leaves actions unchanged.
texts.text: S f = 1 : Leaves actions unchanged.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This perturbation S f is chosen close to 1 to keep stability of the algorithm. For example:
texts.text: This perturbation S f is chosen close to 1 to keep stability of the algorithm. For example:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · S f = 1 . 1 if stock score is 5 and a t > 0 or score is 1 and a t < 0
texts.text: S f = 1 . 1 if stock score is 5 and a t > 0 or score is 1 and a t < 0

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · S f = 1 . 05 if stock score is 4 and a t > 0 or score is 2 and a t < 0
texts.text: S f = 1 . 05 if stock score is 4 and a t > 0 or score is 2 and a t < 0

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · S f = 0 . 95 if stock score is 4 and a t < 0 or score is 2 and a t > 0
texts.text: S f = 0 . 95 if stock score is 4 and a t < 0 or score is 2 and a t > 0

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · S f = 0 . 9 if stock score is 5 and a t < 0 or score is 1 and a t > 0
texts.text: S f = 0 . 9 if stock score is 5 and a t < 0 or score is 1 and a t > 0

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4.3. LLM-infused CVaR-PPO(CPPO)
texts.text: 4.3. LLM-infused CVaR-PPO(CPPO)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Financial news from the FNSPID dataset is processed with an LLM to generate risk scores ( R i f ) for each stock i and day.
texts.text: Financial news from the FNSPID dataset is processed with an LLM to generate risk scores ( R i f ) for each stock i and day.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The perturbation R i f is also chosen close to 1 to keep stability of the algorithm. For example:
texts.text: The perturbation R i f is also chosen close to 1 to keep stability of the algorithm. For example:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · R i f = 1 . 1 for very high risk score 5
texts.text: R i f = 1 . 1 for very high risk score 5

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · R i f = 1 . 05 for high risk score 4
texts.text: R i f = 1 . 05 for high risk score 4

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · R i f = 1 for moderate risk score 3
texts.text: R i f = 1 for moderate risk score 3

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · R i f = 0 . 95 for low risk score 2
texts.text: R i f = 0 . 95 for low risk score 2

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · R i f = 0 . 9 for very low risk score 1
texts.text: R i f = 0 . 9 for very low risk score 1

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The aggregate risk score R f is defined as:
texts.text: The aggregate risk score R f is defined as:

texts.label: formula
texts.orig: R f = ∑ i w i R i f

texts.label: text
texts.orig: where w i is the financial weight of stock i in the portfolio (we have: ∑ i w i = 1 ).
texts.text: where w i is the financial weight of stock i in the portfolio (we have: ∑ i w i = 1 ).

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Figure 1. Backtesting after 500k training steps, 3 years training history, 1 year trading
texts.text: Figure 1. Backtesting after 500k training steps, 3 years training history, 1 year trading

texts.label: text
texts.orig: These scores adjust trajectory returns in Cvar PPO to reflect market risk. The adjusted return is defined as:
texts.text: These scores adjust trajectory returns in Cvar PPO to reflect market risk. The adjusted return is defined as:

texts.label: formula
texts.orig: D R f ( π θ ) = R f · D ( π θ )

texts.label: text
texts.orig: At the end, financial news influence trading actions in two ways: through S f and R f .
texts.text: At the end, financial news influence trading actions in two ways: through S f and R f .

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 6. Conclusion
texts.text: 6. Conclusion

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We propose LLM-infused RL agents for algorithmic trading that integrates stock trading recommendations and risk assessments from news. Future work includes the following directions:
texts.text: We propose LLM-infused RL agents for algorithmic trading that integrates stock trading recommendations and risk assessments from news. Future work includes the following directions:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Optimizing RAM usage: Longer training requires more RAM. For instance, 500k training steps used 16 GB (+Swap memory), whereas 2 million steps required 128 GB (+Swap memory). Optimizing memory efficiency is crucial for scalability.
texts.text: Optimizing RAM usage: Longer training requires more RAM. For instance, 500k training steps used 16 GB (+Swap memory), whereas 2 million steps required 128 GB (+Swap memory). Optimizing memory efficiency is crucial for scalability.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Reducing timescale: Since markets react quickly and abruptly to events, reducing the timescale of decisionmaking could improve trading performance.
texts.text: Reducing timescale: Since markets react quickly and abruptly to events, reducing the timescale of decisionmaking could improve trading performance.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Improving news signal quality: Enhancing the quality of news signals from the FNSPID dataset is an important direction to ensure better market performance.
texts.text: Improving news signal quality: Enhancing the quality of news signals from the FNSPID dataset is an important direction to ensure better market performance.
